page: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 480 MB L3 cache, and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz. Local storage is provided via a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with the Geekbench single-core score and text processing tests placing in the top tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong results in the top 25 percent. However, multi-core scaling presents a tradeoff; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 25 percent. Memory bandwidth is mixed, with small-block cached reads scoring in the bottom 25 percent, while large-block read and write operations maintain average performance.

This instance is designed for workloads that benefit from high single-core clock speeds and fast local NVMe storage, such as single-threaded processing, text manipulation, and lightweight database operations. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation offer a compute-dense profile, though the 8.0 GB memory capacity limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications. It is appropriate for static web serving, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference, though text generation on larger models is constrained by the hardware limits.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this configuration delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 10 percent, alongside strong single-threaded compression results. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth present tradeoffs, with stress-ng multi-core and small-block memory read benchmarks falling into the bottom 25 percent. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is suited for single-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that leverage fast local storage, while remaining constrained for memory-intensive or highly parallelized multi-core workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance with multi-core tradeoffs.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and integrated NVMe SSD local storage for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance storage.
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression capabilities.
Tradeoffs in multi-core scaling and cached memory bandwidth.
Appropriate for software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
